plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and stability.
Reduce QQQ to 15% to manage overall broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend and to lower sector concentration.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting a potential ~1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, prioritizing stability over aggressive growth. Expect lower drawdowns but also more modest returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and manage broad market risk.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its 'SELL' flag and high risk; trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to 15% and 10% respectively due to their downtrends and 'TRIM' flags.
Add ZS at 8% for its strong scanner read and risk/reward profile.
Initiate a position in NVDA at 8% as a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner score.
These moves balance broad market exposure with quality tech and reduced leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting a potential ~2.5% monthly return. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing high-quality tech setups. Expect moderate drawdowns with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL completely due to its 'SELL' flag; keep SPAL at 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to 10% each due to their downtrends and 'TRIM' flags.
Buy ZS at 15% to lean into its highest scanner score and strong risk/reward.
Initiate a position in NVDA at 15% to concentrate in high-scoring, quality tech.
This plan uses leverage and concentration in top setups for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets a potential ~4% monthly return by leaning into leverage and concentration in top-scoring setups. Be aware that this approach significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown potential. Leveraged ETFs experience daily decay and rebalancing, leading to substantial losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to manage concentration while retaining broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, prioritizing low drawdown.
Increase QQQI to 35% to heavily favor income and monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% due to its downtrend and to shift focus to income.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single-name exposure.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend and to minimize growth-oriented sector exposure.
income.read: This income plan aims to park cash for yield, targeting a potential ~1% monthly distribution. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth by rotating into income-focused ETFs. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is income-focused, not a savings account.
